meta.chroma:document:
arch-btw — v1 shared understanding (interview-grilled with pasha, 2026-06-24)

WHAT IT IS: Single-player portfolio + personal learning tool (Q1 = A and C equally). No strangers, no payments, no multi-tenancy. Complexity budget goes to critique quality and canvas feel, NOT infra.

CORE LOOP: Pick a problem -> optionally ask clarifying questions (interviewer answers from canned facts) -> design on an infinite tldraw canvas (drag prefab boxes, connect with directed arrows, explain each box, keep a global "train of thought" notes section) -> TURN-BASED PR-REVIEW LOOP ("Request review" -> reviewer probes -> you edit diagram AND/OR type a reply -> press button to re-review -> repeat) -> "Finish & get verdict" -> structured debrief. Chess-turn / PR-review metaphor: explicit turns, NOT free-flowing live chat.

THE REVIEWER: Behaves like a London startup senior engineer / hiring manager, NOT FAANG-algorithmic. Judges HOW you think and justify yourself, not blueprint-matching. Hybrid grounding (Q2=C): hidden per-problem rubric + hidden reference keep it sharp and consistent, but weird-but-valid designs still pass. Interactive interview ending in a verdict (Q3=C).

COMPONENTS (Q4/Q5/Q6): ~12 prefab box types + Custom box. Each box has an OPTIONAL freeform explanation field; an empty field is meaningful signal the reviewer can probe. Also a design-level "train of thought" notes section. NO typed property schemas (rejected as too much). Palette: Service/API, Worker, Queue/Stream, SQL DB, NoSQL, Cache, Object storage, Gateway/LB, Third-party API, LLM provider, Webhook source, Cron, + Custom. (Vector DB / Auth provider are trivial later additions.)

PROBLEM MODEL (Q8): id, title, difficulty, tags, prompt (deliberately vague), canned clarification facts (revealed when asked), hidden rubric, hidden reference design. Reference REVEALED in debrief framed as "one strong approach" (Q8=B). Authored LLM-drafted + human-curated, stored as STATIC SERVER-ONLY TS files so rubric/reference never reach the browser (Q9=B). v1 = 6 curated problems (2 easy / 2 medium / 2 hard), all available from start, labeled by difficulty (no gating).

SESSION FLOW (Q7=B): Briefing (optional clarification chat) -> Design -> turn-based Review loop -> Debrief. User controls when interview ends via "Finish & get verdict" (Q16=C). Soft wind-down ~5-7 turns, hard cap ~10 to bound cost. Per-turn output = conversational review comments; final output = structured scorecard.

SERIALIZATION (Q10): tldraw store -> clean { nodes, edges, notes } JSON via a deliberately-tested adapter (first-class module — critique quality depends on it). Directed arrows. Structured JSON, NOT image/multimodal. Full snapshot re-sent each turn (no diffing). Store BOTH tldraw native snapshot (for reload/edit) and derived graph JSON (LLM input + durable record) in the attempt row.

CANVAS LIB (Q11): tldraw (user chose over React Flow; doesn't care about watermark, likes the company). Consequence accepted: we own custom ShapeUtils per component type (with explanation prop) + the store->graph extraction adapter.

DEBRIEF (Q12=C): generateObject + Zod scorecard. Per-rubric ratings + comments, one-line verdict (advance / borderline / not yet), 2-3 transcript callbacks ("when I pushed on X you said Y"), expandable revealed reference. Kept tight, not a wall of text.

LLM (Q13): Vercel AI SDK, swappable across EXACTLY Gemini / Anthropic / OpenAI, selected by whichever of GEMINI_API_KEY / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / OPENAI_API_KEY is present. All calls server-side, browser never sees a key. streamText for review turns, generateObject for debrief. Strong reasoning model default (critique quality is the whole product).

AUTH (Q14): Better Auth (user dislikes Auth.js as finicky) + GitHub OAuth. Self-hosted, owns its tables via Drizzle adapter.

DATA (Q15=A): Neon Postgres + Drizzle ORM (over Prisma/Turso). Attempts table stores tldraw snapshot + graph JSON + transcript + scorecard + verdict + userId + problemId + createdAt (JSON-heavy). Problems NOT in DB (static server-only files). TYPE SPINE: Drizzle (DB) + Zod (scorecard, shared by AI SDK generateObject / DB JSON column / React render) + static typed problem files. One source of truth per concern.

STACK (Q17): Next.js App Router (latest stable, React 19) + TS strict, Route Handlers for AI SDK streaming, Tailwind + shadcn/ui (polish matters), Vercel hosting. Layout: tldraw canvas center, problem briefing + review thread side panel.

OUT OF SCOPE v1: voice chat (parked as future), runtime problem generation, multi-tenancy, payments, typed component property schemas.

USER PREFERENCES OBSERVED: spec-driven, TS-fluent, pragmatic about scope, wants honest critical feedback over agreement. Dislikes Auth.js. Likes Better Auth + tldraw.
